title: Out-of-bounds Write in FortiOS SSL-VPN service
description: An out-of-bounds write vulnerability in FortiOS sslvpn may allow a remote attacker to execute arbitrary code or commands via specially crafted HTTP requests.

title: Format String Vulnerability in fgfm daemon
description: A format string vulnerability in FortiOS fgfm daemon may allow a remote unauthenticated attacker to execute arbitrary code or commands via specially crafted packets sent to port 541.
workaround: Restrict access to port 541 using Local-In policies.

title: Heap-based Buffer Overflow in SSLVPN Daemon
description: A heap-based buffer overflow vulnerability in FortiOS sslvpn daemon may allow a remote unauthenticated attacker to execute arbitrary code or commands via specially crafted requests.

title: Heap-based Buffer Overflow in sslvpn daemon (Active Exploitation)
description: A heap-based buffer overflow vulnerability in FortiOS sslvpn daemon may allow a remote unauthenticated attacker to execute arbitrary code or commands via specially crafted TLS requests.

title: Authentication Bypass in Administrative Interface
description: An authentication bypass using an alternate path or channel in FortiOS administrative interface may allow a remote unauthenticated attacker to perform administrative actions via specially crafted HTTP/HTTPS requests.
workaround: Restrict administrative access to trusted hosts only.

title: Out-of-bounds Write in SSL VPN Daemon
description: An out-of-bounds write vulnerability in FortiOS SSL VPN daemon may allow a remote unauthenticated attacker to execute arbitrary code or commands via specially crafted HTTP requests.

title: SSL VPN Authentication Bypass
description: An authentication bypass vulnerability in FortiOS SSL VPN daemon may allow a remote attacker to log in without MFA or credentials under specific circumstances.
